title: The Safe House

jokes.text: I just want to get some chubby hubby. Sir, could you please give us a moment?
jokes.setup: Larry repeatedly trying to get ice cream while a woman is crying being comforted
jokes.punchline: His persistence in wanting 'chubby hubby' ice cream during an emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David - obliviously prioritizing his minor wants over others' major emotional moments. The specificity of 'chubby hubby' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Don't pay attention to that jerk.
jokes.setup: Margaret comforting crying woman after Larry's ice cream interruption
jokes.punchline: Calling Larry a jerk when he was just trying to buy ice cream
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Larry as the misunderstood antagonist. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Richard is dating a burlesque dancer. I don't like to sound like a pig, but she has magnificent breasts. She's dumb.
jokes.setup: Trying not to sound like a pig while discussing Richard's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Immediately objectifies her breasts then dismisses her intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic male character hypocrisy - claiming not to be a pig while being exactly that. The blunt delivery works.

jokes.text: I was on the phone with an ex-girlfriend who was suicidal, so I couldn't exactly hang up to say I want to go to a salad bar.
jokes.setup: Lewis explaining why he's late
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposing suicide prevention with wanting to go to a salad bar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the casual way he mentions suicide alongside the mundane desire for salad. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: A lot of people call me who are suicidal. I don't think you'd be my suicide call.
jokes.setup: Lewis claiming many suicidal people call him
jokes.punchline: Larry wouldn't be someone's suicide call - too unsympathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Larry's lack of empathy is so well-known even his friends acknowledge it. Dark but character-driven.

jokes.text: So you'd be calling the guy who caused you to-- Okay.
jokes.setup: Learning that Lewis would call him if suicidal because Larry caused the problem
jokes.punchline: Larry realizing the circular logic of calling your tormentor for help
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Larry processing uncomfortable truths about himself. The trailing off works well.

jokes.text: Hey, if it wasn't for fucking burlesque, we wouldn't have Chaplin or the Marx brothers. Oh, Chaplin was a great pole dancer.
jokes.setup: Lewis defending burlesque as legitimate art form citing famous comedians
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically imagining Chaplin as a pole dancer
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual absurdity - imagining the dignified Charlie Chaplin pole dancing. The juxtaposition is perfect and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Have you set a date aside when you're going to finally look at her face?
jokes.setup: Lewis defending dating the burlesque dancer for more than physical reasons
jokes.punchline: Larry asking when Lewis will actually look at her face
jokes.explanation: Brutally direct Larry line that cuts through Lewis's pretense. The specificity of 'set a date aside' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She gives to charity. Milk? She gives milk?
jokes.setup: Lewis saying his girlfriend gives to charity
jokes.punchline: Larry misunderstanding and thinking she gives milk (breast milk)
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection playing on Larry's fixation with her breasts. The confusion feels natural for the character.

jokes.text: Dance for underprivileged kids. Charity! Gives to charity?
jokes.setup: Clarifying what kind of charity work she does
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of burlesque dancing as charity work for kids
jokes.explanation: Pays off the charity confusion but the premise is a bit thin. Works as escalation of the absurdity.

jokes.text: Computer watching chain of responsibility
jokes.setup: Larry asks someone to watch his computer, that person has to leave
jokes.punchline: Chain of computer watching that will lead to it being stolen
jokes.explanation: Great setup for future complications. The domino effect of trust being passed along strangers is very Curb-like.

jokes.text: No bag? Where's your bag? Because the dog without the bag-- It's incomplete. It's a marriage. The bag and the dog, they go together.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting dog owner about not cleaning up
jokes.punchline: His philosophical explanation of the dog-bag relationship as a 'marriage'
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry David - taking a simple concept and creating elaborate philosophical justification. 'It's a marriage' is brilliantly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm yelling for society, for everybody.
jokes.setup: Saundra asking why Larry had to yell at her
jokes.punchline: Larry positioning himself as society's representative in dog poop matters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry grandiosity - elevating his personal pet peeve to a societal crusade. The self-righteousness is perfectly calibrated.

jokes.text: It's like jell-o pudding inside of those motherfuckers. The fuckin' titties are talking right now. Those titties are a blabbermouth.
jokes.setup: Leon watching the burlesque show
jokes.punchline: His elaborate metaphorical description of breasts as communicative jello
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon absurdity - anthropomorphizing breasts with elaborate food metaphors. 'Titties are a blabbermouth' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Those titties are a blabbermouth, you know what I'm talking about? They're fuckin' just yippity-yapping right now.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what the breasts are saying
jokes.punchline: Leon's anthropomorphizing of breasts as chatty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon absurdism - turning anatomy into personality with vivid language

jokes.text: Those titties are a blabbermouth, you know what I'm talking about? They're fuckin' just yippity-yapping right now.
jokes.setup: The talking breasts concept
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor with 'blabbermouth' and 'yippity-yapping'
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Leon dialogue - the personification of breasts as chatty is perfectly absurd and memorable

jokes.text: I thought you said he was old. You don't seem that old. You called me old? You're two days older than I am.
jokes.setup: Stella commenting Larry doesn't seem as old as Lewis described
jokes.punchline: Lewis calling Larry old when he's barely older himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing petty competitiveness between friends. The specificity of 'two days' makes it feel real.

jokes.text: You're a dissipated alcoholic. How dare you? Recovering alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Larry getting offended about being called old
jokes.punchline: The correction from alcoholic to 'recovering' alcoholic as if that makes it better
jokes.explanation: The technical correction misses the point of the insult. Good escalation of their petty fight.

jokes.text: I saw the movie 'Gypsy' with Natalie Wood. Oh, then you should be a Professor at Ohio state and teach this.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming knowledge of burlesque from one movie
jokes.punchline: Stella's sarcastic suggestion he could be a professor based on one movie
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback. The specificity of Ohio State is random but works for the dismissive tone.

jokes.text: Did you like the air fucking number I did? The air fucking was sensational.
jokes.setup: Stella casually asking about her sexual dance routine
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact praise of 'air fucking' as if it's high art
jokes.explanation: Larry treating explicit performance like legitimate theater criticism. The clinical tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I did notice in the show that there's a small mole on the underside of your right breast that you might want to get checked out. How did you see a small mole from where you were sitting?
jokes.setup: Larry offering medical advice about a mole he spotted during the show
jokes.punchline: Lewis questioning how Larry could see such detail from the audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for future complications. Larry's inappropriate attention to detail reveals his true focus while seeming helpful.

jokes.text: I have breast vision and that's how I'm able to-- You have breast vision?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how he spotted the mole
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have 'breast vision' like a superpower
jokes.explanation: Fantastic absurd concept - breast vision as a superpower. Perfectly captures Larry's shameless objectification while making it ridiculous.

jokes.text: I could be a burlesque dermatologist.
jokes.setup: Building on the breast vision concept
jokes.punchline: Larry imagining a career combining his 'talent' with medicine
jokes.explanation: Great callback that extends the breast vision concept logically. The specificity of 'burlesque dermatologist' is perfect.

jokes.text: I gave it to some black guy. You gave it to some black guy. You gave it to a black guy?! What I was about to say was why wouldn't you give it to a black person?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining who he gave the computer to
jokes.punchline: The racist implication followed by Larry's awkward attempt to seem progressive
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb racial awkwardness - Larry trying to backpedal makes it worse. The overcompensation is perfectly cringey.

jokes.text: You trusted that black motherfucker. I could've stole this motherfucker... Plenty of times, but I didn't. I know the code to the fuckin' house and shit. 3948, right? The last four of your social security card number is 4938.
jokes.setup: Leon upset about racial profiling in the computer theft
jokes.punchline: Proving his trustworthiness by revealing he knows all Larry's security information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon logic - proving trustworthiness by demonstrating how easily he could rob Larry. The specific details make it funnier.

jokes.text: Your signature-- Come on. It's a big L. It's real clear, right? Then the 'arry David' is sloppy. Any man can do that shit. Your mom's maiden name-- Ratner.
jokes.setup: Leon continuing to list Larry's security vulnerabilities
jokes.punchline: Detailed knowledge of Larry's signature style and mother's maiden name
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Leon has WAY too much access to Larry's personal information. The signature analysis is particularly good.

jokes.text: Some people call them battered women. I choose not to use that phrase. Oh, they're battered. I think it's very pejorative.
jokes.setup: Margaret explaining her preferred terminology for abuse victims
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately using the 'pejorative' term she just avoided
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's complete inability to read social cues about sensitive language.

jokes.text: So it would be so great to have a safe man in their midst that could give them a male perspective that they could feel good about hearing. So I would be representing men.
jokes.setup: Margaret suggesting Larry could provide positive male perspective to abuse victims
jokes.punchline: The irony of Larry being chosen as a 'safe' male representative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - audience knows Larry is the worst possible choice for this role. Sets up future disasters perfectly.

jokes.text: Maybe I could drum up a date over there, huh? Oh, now Larry, let's remember boundaries.
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing to help at the women's shelter
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggesting he might find romantic prospects there
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness - treating a shelter for abuse victims as a dating pool. Margaret's understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't know where you were living before, but I would think this probably would be a step up. You hit the jackpot with this place, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry complimenting the shelter house
jokes.punchline: Suggesting abuse victims 'hit the jackpot' by escaping to a shelter
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry tone-deafness - treating escape from abuse as winning the lottery. Shows his complete lack of awareness.

jokes.text: My father would call this crap. That's a lot of crap.
jokes.setup: Larry looking at the shelter's food spread
jokes.punchline: Calling donated food 'crap' using his father's judgment
jokes.explanation: Another inappropriate Larry comment, but less inspired than others. The father reference adds some character depth.

jokes.text: I was just going to say th your anger is your problem and it's not my problem anymore. Huh, interesting.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing to dog owner for yelling at her
jokes.punchline: Saundra dismissing his apology with therapy-speak, Larry's confused reaction
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - the therapy language clashes with Larry's expectation of normal social interaction.

jokes.text: Do you want to apologize to my dog? Because you really yelled at my dog. Yeah, it's very hard to apologize to a dog because they're a stupid animal.
jokes.setup: Saundra asking Larry to apologize to the dog
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt refusal based on dogs being 'stupid animals'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - refusing to apologize to a dog because it's too stupid to understand. Brutally logical and inappropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's very hard to apologize to a dog because they're a stupid animal.
jokes.setup: Being asked to apologize to a dog
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt refusal with insulting reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - doubling down with more offensive statements

jokes.text: I would go maybe to the-- Where the Japanese food is. The hoisin sauce I don't think is a big-- It's not like buying ice cream.
jokes.setup: Larry giving advice on where to cry in grocery stores
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the Asian food aisle because hoisin sauce isn't popular
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the original incident. The specificity of hoisin sauce shows Larry's analytical approach to social problems.

jokes.text: What a bovine she is, huh? She's like the capo here, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Dale to Margaret
jokes.punchline: Calling an abuse victim 'bovine' and asking if she's the mafia boss
jokes.explanation: Outrageous Larry comment combining animal comparison with mafia terminology for an abuse victim. Perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: Does she run the show? Tell me the truth. Is this the inspirational speaker that we're getting?
jokes.setup: Asking about Dale's role
jokes.punchline: Assuming the large woman must be the inspirational speaker
jokes.explanation: Larry's assumptions based on appearance continue his inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: If you need a machine, you're welcome to come and use my machine anytime you want. Give me a little round of applause if you want to. It won't kill you. Go ahead. That's kind of tepid.
jokes.setup: Larry offering his washing machine to the shelter
jokes.punchline: Demanding applause and critiquing the enthusiasm level
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry narcissism - doing something nice then demanding praise and critiquing the response. The progression is perfect.

jokes.text: Give me a little round of applause if you want to. It won't kill you. Go ahead. That's kind of tepid.
jokes.setup: Making a generous offer
jokes.punchline: Demanding and then criticizing their applause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry ego - turning charity into opportunity for praise and criticism

jokes.text: Did you put one of these disgusting things back in here after eating it? There's a whole side dish here for the remnants. This is the remnant plate.
jokes.setup: Finding partially eaten food
jokes.punchline: Larry's detailed food etiquette lecture
jokes.explanation: Larry's obsession with proper food protocol in inappropriate setting

jokes.text: Imagine you had huge balls. Enormous, sweaty, just filled with spunk-- You're comparing breasts with balls? People hate balls. Balls are reviled.
jokes.setup: Stella trying to explain breast pain using male anatomy
jokes.punchline: Larry's outrage at comparing beloved breasts to hated balls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gender comedy - the passionate defense of breasts vs. hatred of balls. Larry's indignation is perfectly calibrated.

jokes.text: Balls are male boobs. What? Balls are disgusting they're hideous. Everybody wants to see those. Who wants to see balls?
jokes.setup: Stella's equivalency argument
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly frantic differentiation between body parts
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the anatomy debate. 'Balls are male boobs' is an unexpectedly profound observation delivered absurdly.

jokes.text: You take an advil for that stuff. You throw a couple of stones behind your brassiere. You just even it out. Like a seesaw.
jokes.setup: Stella's back pain from large breasts
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous engineering solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd practical solution with the seesaw comparison being perfect

jokes.text: Imagine you had huge balls. Enormous, sweaty, just filled with spunk--
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the burden of large breasts
jokes.punchline: The graphic comparison to male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Escalating gross-out humor with anatomical comparison

jokes.text: Maybe half.
jokes.setup: Being asked how much she's taking off
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of removing half her breasts
jokes.explanation: Casual delivery of shocking information drives the men's panic

jokes.text: You looked at her mole with a goddamn telescope like Galileo jerking off from 40' away.
jokes.setup: Lewis blaming Larry for the breast reduction decision
jokes.punchline: Comparing Larry to Galileo masturbating with a telescope
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant metaphor combining scientific achievement with perverted observation. This is a potential cultural reference line.

jokes.text: What's half of double d? B+? No, B-. What's closer to the D, the minus or the plus?
jokes.setup: Calculating breast reduction mathematics
jokes.punchline: Treating cup sizes like academic grades with precise calculations
jokes.explanation: Larry's analytical approach to intimate topics - turning romance into mathematics

jokes.text: There shouldn't be a motherfucker cutting titties down. You don't fuckin' wanna reduce your titties by half and expect a motherfucker to be happy with that shit.
jokes.setup: Discussion of breast reduction surgery
jokes.punchline: Leon's passionate opposition using his distinctive profanity-laden style
jokes.explanation: Pure Leon philosophy delivered with characteristic passion. The repetition of 'motherfucker' creates rhythm.

jokes.text: You say that to white people too. You can't turn blue? Hell no I can't turn blue. Blacks don't fuckin' blush. Never seen a black blush in my fuckin' life.
jokes.setup: Larry using expression 'talk till we're blue in the face'
jokes.punchline: Leon's scientific analysis of racial differences in color changes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant racial comedy that's educational and absurd. Leon's matter-of-fact delivery of biological observations is perfect.

jokes.text: You don't fuckin' wanna reduce your titties by half and expect a motherfucker to be happy with that shit.
jokes.setup: The breast reduction discussion
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Pure Leon voice expressing the men's collective outrage

jokes.text: So, Dale, if you don't mind my saying, with all due respect, I was just wondering how you kind of... You know, wound up over there. Because it looks like you could... Take care of yourself.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to delicately ask about Dale's abuse situation
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to ask sensitively while suggesting she looks tough
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's complete lack of tact when dealing with sensitive subjects. Sets up the reveal.

jokes.text: [Dale reveals black eye] That is some shiner you got there.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Dale's presence at the shelter
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal of severe black eye, Larry's casual reaction
jokes.explanation: Strong visual reveal that pays off the setup. Larry's casual 'shiner' comment shows his inability to respond appropriately to serious situations.

jokes.text: Dale punches Larry giving him black eye
jokes.setup: Larry's insensitive questioning about Dale's abuse
jokes.punchline: Dale violently proves she can both give and take abuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Larry's insensitive probing - physical comedy that proves his assumptions wrong

jokes.text: That is some shiner you got there. Can you tell me how it happened? I was home and I was talking to somebody. And... I said something I shouldn't have said. So this person struck you? No. I fell. Yeah, I fell. I'm clumsy.
jokes.setup: Larry investigating Dale's black eye
jokes.punchline: The classic domestic violence denial pattern
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in the familiar pattern of domestic violence denial

jokes.text: You know, I didn't even ask him to move in. He just moved in, doesn't pay any rent and he eats all my food. He knows all about me. He knows my mother's maiden name. I've got to get this guy out of my house.
jokes.setup: Explaining his relationship with Leon to Dale
jokes.punchline: The summary of Leon's complete takeover of his life
jokes.explanation: Summary of the Leon situation that sets up the final twist

jokes.text: You know, I didn't even ask him to move in. He just moved in, doesn't pay any rent and he eats all my food. He knows all about me. He knows my mother's maiden name. I've got to get this guy out of my house.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining Leon to Dale
jokes.punchline: Listing all of Leon's boundary violations while asking abuse victim for advice
jokes.explanation: Great callback that ironically parallels Larry's situation with Leon to domestic abuse. The irony of asking an abuse victim for help is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm going to throw myself and those breasts a farewell party like they've never seen in their lives. A breast blowout.
jokes.setup: Lewis planning for Stella's breast reduction surgery
jokes.punchline: Throwing a farewell party specifically for the breasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - treating body parts as separate entities worthy of their own farewell party. 'Breast blowout' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: So on Thursday I'm going to throw myself and those breasts a farewell party like they've never seen in their lives. A breast blowout.
jokes.setup: Stella's upcoming breast reduction surgery
jokes.punchline: Lewis planning an elaborate goodbye party for breasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Lewis's breast obsession into formal farewell ceremony

jokes.text: It's like the last time you're going to see them, like going off to war. I never went to 'nam and I feel like this is sort of like-- In a way sort of the same thing for me.
jokes.setup: Lewis explaining the significance of the breast farewell party
jokes.punchline: Comparing breast reduction to Vietnam War experience
jokes.explanation: Outrageous comparison between losing access to breasts and war trauma. The self-awareness makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: So you think that all black people look alike? I think all computers look alike.
jokes.setup: Accusation of racial profiling
jokes.punchline: Larry's deflection to computer similarity instead of addressing race
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deflection - avoiding racial discussion with absurd logic about computer similarity. Brilliant misdirection and economy.

jokes.text: You match the description of somebody who's been reported for domestic battery at this address.
jokes.setup: Bob being arrested at Larry's house
jokes.punchline: Computer return guy mistaken for Larry the domestic abuser
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation bringing together multiple plot threads with innocent man arrested for Larry's actions

jokes.text: [Police arrest wrong person as Larry returns computer] You're going to jail now. A computer. I don't live here. You're making a mistake. Wait for the guy to come back.
jokes.setup: Bob trying to return stolen computer gets mistaken for abusive boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Perfect ironic reversal where good deed leads to arrest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Curb payoff - all storylines collide in ironic disaster. The good Samaritan gets punished while Larry escapes consequences.

tmdb_overview: Larry becomes an unlikely role model for battered women. Richard Lewis's relationship with a burlesque performer is put to the test. Leon survives a case of mistaken identity.
